embeds.title: Deixis
embeds.description: In linguistics, deixis () is the use of words or phrases to refer to a particular time (e.g. then), place (e.g. here), or person (e.g. you) relative to the context of the utterance. Deixis exists in all known natural languages and is closely related to anaphora, with a sometimes unclear distinction

okay so (and yes I should have asked *before* I submitted the work) I wanted y'alls thoughts on how to word this Posse. I figured this would be good enough, but playing with it more I realize I made a minor lapse in judgment. 

The problem I see with this now is that the verb "Unleash" is a performative keyword—that is to say, every instance of the use of the word Unleash in a sequence of actions can be tracked and have consequences on other effects. The separation of the verbs Discover and Unleash here insinuates that two Posses are being Unleashed (the one that lets you Discover, then the one you pick out of 3) when that's not true.

Okay so my vibes-based approach continues to fail me, Lens please help tell me what I'm talking about here:

The summary is that we need whatever sentence structure and word choice we pick for Aliemus to consistently align with the other words that cards use:

__Deal__ X DMG.
__Gain__ X Shield.
__Inflict__ X Poison.

"Gain" Aliemus is fine if it's the Awakener's card giving the Aliemus to themselves, but in the case of things like Posses, Relics, or cards like Short-Term Memory, "Gain" suddenly becomes incorrect to use **in the same way that we wouln't word a card "Enemy takes X DMG".**
Fresh, Awakener-sourced Aliemus are the only kind of Aliemus that go through the Aliemus% increasing belt
-# (Awakener-and-their-equipment-sourced Aliemus)
<@1376763322568937562> if you're not actively following along I feel like I made a good point here, to loop you back in

It might be difficult to concisely convey which is which, and the only possible thing that comes to mind is...

Thais grants herself X Aliemis.
Thais grants herself and the target Y Alienus.
Thais grants all Awakeners Z Aluemus.

I think fundamentally we just need to communicate to the designers that we feel like there is an issue where awakener-sourced aliemus increase is not differentiated from static unattributed aliemus increases, at least in how the text conveys the effects

I never got around to this because there were way too many buttons that had the same text on them that it was risky to determine which one was which, but I think it would look nice to customize more of the buttons in line with the verbiage used for each thing.

Posse: `Release` -> `Unleash`
Keyflare Rouse: `Release` -> `Perform` / `Rouse`
Exalt: `Confirm` -> `Release`
Annihilation: `Confirm` -> `Perform` / `Annihilate`
Lineup menu: `Quick Team Lineup` -> `Quick Deploy`, `Investigation` -> `Investigate`
Crimson Furnace: `Confirm` -> `Consume`
Rouse menu: `Select Ability` -> `Select an Awakener to Rouse`, `Select` -> `Rouse`
D-Mark Orison menu: `Confirm` -> `Engrave`
I don't agree with most of these, truthfully, but I can't really put my finger on why, it feels off to think about clicking "Unleash" instead of clicking "Confirm" on a menu.

In general we strive to implement improvements while keeping the translation stable;
Sometimes we see suggestions but may not be able to implement it immediately, for example last time we were planning to implement the "gain Aliemus" -> "increase Aliemus" change, but it got shot down by combat design people as they apparently had a reason to use "gain" in CN, not sure, i'll follow up more

Actually, I'm going to follow up on this since I just figured out what felt off.

Our job is simply to translate. We are not to add embellishments to the game unless the original text is also to be changed... If it says "Confirm" in CN then it should be "Confirm" in EN.

The suggestion could be brought up to the staff at BIAV instead, though... that's probably more of a game suggestion and would be put into the suggestion/bug-report forum channel in the official server.

It was a English-specific issue, where Active/Passive Voice is more important and should be given some consideration

I think there's a bit of confusion here

other way around.
Passive Voice would be "Castor *gains* Aliemus", because the performer of the action is *unclear*.
(The action here being the generation of Aliemus.)
Active Voice would be "__Increase__ Castor's Aliemus", because the performer of the action is __important__.
Active Voice is needed in this scenario because Aliemus% (like Incalculable Factor) is a buff to the performer, not the receiver.
active and passive are not the correct terms for this, technically

to quote my original post on the matter

>>> also I'm nitpicking myself because i kind of used the word object to simplify it but technically speaking from a grammatical point of view it's the indirect object so I kind of need to clarify that just to feel better

anyway, to visualize it a bit better:

"thais grants target awakener 10 aliemus" > subject = thais, object = aliemus, indirect object = target awakener

in this situation, if thais has 20% aliemus stat, it becomes 12. This makes intuitive sense because thais is the subject, i.e., the person performing the action of granting, and thus her stats should modify the effect of that action. Target awakener, as the beneficiary, simply receives the aliemus and their stats are irrelevant 

"target awakener accrues 10 aliemus" > subject = target awakener, object = aliemus. 

In this case, although Thais is the source from which this effect originated, she is not involved in the sentence structure at all. If Thais has 20% aliemus and it becomes 12, why is the stat being modified? The action is being carried out by target awakener, while the source of the values comes from an unmentioned third party whose stats actually determine the effect yet is not referenced in the grammatical structure of the sentence

honestly its a bit of a confusing thing to word while making clear how gameplay factors affect it
like "grant target 10 aliemus" sounds natural but leaves out who does it, but including "thais grants target 10 aliemus" sounds weird on a card
but yeah this might be kind of difficult to explain to chinese devs
because transitivity of verbs does exist in other languages naturally, and therefore also in chinese
the problem is that the grammatical structure of chinese doesn't have direct verb conjugations
so a transitive form of a verb is in fact the same as the intransitive
The only reasonable course of action here is to abolish the English language altogether <:murphy_cake:1408324295180030002>
the transitivity is determined by the existence of an indirect object
so I think the CN devs likely just might not quite understand it's an issue in english
this would be a lot easier in japanese ironically enough since they generally have different transitive vs intransitive verb forms
actually thinking about it english has a lot of verbs that can be both as well
not sure if I understand the issue enough, but maybe it'd help if the different types of aliemus granting had specific verbs associated with them?

well yeah that was the root issue, our goal is to differentiate the types via separate specific verbs so that the difference is clear
